name: OAuthLib (Generic OAuth 1.0/2.0 Framework for Python)

auth.notes: The library itself implements OAuth — used to build OAuth clients and servers

performance.notes: Pure Python; minimal overhead beyond HTTP requests

agent_readiness.notes: Low-level OAuth 1.0/2.0 framework used by requests-oauthlib and other libraries. from oauthlib.oauth2 import BackendApplicationClient, WebApplicationClient. client = BackendApplicationClient(client_id=CLIENT_ID). token = client.prepare_request_body(). URI, headers, body = client.add_token(url). OAuth1: OAuth1Session (via requests-oauthlib). Typically used via requests-oauthlib rather than directly. oauthlib.oauth2.rfc6749 for custom server. Most users should use requests-oauthlib or authlib instead of oauthlib directly.

gotchas: Low-level API — most users should use requests-oauthlib or authlib instead
Server-side OAuth implementation requires deep OAuth 2.0 spec knowledge
Not async — use authlib for async OAuth flows
best_when: Building custom OAuth 2.0 server (authorization server implementation)
When requests-oauthlib wraps it for OAuth 1.0/2.0 client flows
Low-level OAuth protocol implementation for custom integrations
avoid_when: Client-only OAuth flows (use requests-oauthlib or authlib directly)
summary: OAuthLib is the low-level OAuth 1.0/2.0 protocol implementation underlying requests-oauthlib and many other libraries. BackendApplicationClient, WebApplicationClient for OAuth2 flows. Used as dependency by requests-oauthlib. BSD-3. Most users interact via requests-oauthlib (sync) or authlib (async) rather than oauthlib directly. Use for custom OAuth server implementation.
